title: 7 PaymentStream芒聞垄 Direct Customer Profile Functions
body_text:
7	PaymentStream芒聞垄 Direct Customer Profile Functions
7.1	Overview of PaymentStream Direct Customer Profile Functions
To access the functions described in this chapter, your banking system must be connected to
PaymentStream
Direct. The ability to view customers芒聙聶 account information and other account settings allows staff to better serve customers while working in
PaymentStream
Direct (e.g., answer customers芒聙聶 account questions, print statements, print cheque images).
7.2	Accessing a Customer芒聙聶s Profile
To access a customer芒聙聶s profile.
Validate the identity of the customer.
Log in to
PaymentStream
Direct and select the
Transactions
tab if that page isn芒聙聶t automatically displayed.
Enter the membership number or member card number (as determined by the type of login that your financial institution uses) and select
Retrieve Profile
.
See Figure 1.
Note:	A secondary field for the user芒聙聶s branch number (usually a 2- or 3-digit number defined by your financial institution) may be displayed.
Figure 1: Enter Membership Number
The Member Account Summary is displayed on the right side of the page. To initiate tasks on the customer芒聙聶s behalf, select an option from the 芒聙聹Select a Task芒聙聺 drop-down menu or select a link in the navigation menu on the left side of the page. See Figure 2.
Figure 2: View Member Account Summary
To view another profile:
Select
Switch Member
. See Figure 3.
Figure 3: Switch Profile
Enter a membership number or member card number and select
Retrieve Profile
.
7.3	Account Summary
The Account Summary function allows authorized
PaymentStream
Direct users to view customers芒聙聶 account information including balances and scheduled transactions. This function allows
PaymentStream
Direct users to answer customers芒聙聶 account questions while working in
PaymentStream
Direct.
To view a summary of the customer芒聙聶s accounts:
Access the customer芒聙聶s profile as described in Section 7.2, Accessing a Customer芒聙聶s Profile.
Select
View Member芒聙聶s Account Summary
from the 芒聙聹Select a Task芒聙聺 drop-down menu. See Figure 4.
Figure 4: Select the View Member芒聙聶s Account Summary Option
Alternatively, select
Member Profile
in the navigation menu or
View Account Summary
in the Member Account Summary section. This information is always visible while working in a customer-serving state. See Figure 5.
Figure 5: Alternate Access to Account Summary Information
View the customer芒聙聶s account balances and scheduled transactions. See Figure 6.
Note: 	To view the details of a specific account, select the account name. See Section 7.4, Account Activity, for more information.
Figure 6: View Account Summary
7.4	Account Activity
The Account Activity function allows authorized
PaymentStream
Direct users to search for and view account activity on customers芒聙聶 accounts. Access to this information allows
PaymentStream
Direct users to answer customers芒聙聶 account questions while working in
PaymentStream
Direct.
To view the customer芒聙聶s account activity:
Access the customer芒聙聶s profile as described in Section 7.2, Accessing a Customer芒聙聶s Profile.
Select
View Member芒聙聶s Account Activity
from the 芒聙聹Select a Task芒聙聺 drop-down menu. See Figure 7.
Figure 7: Select the View Member芒聙聶s Account Activity Option
Alternatively, select
Member Profile
>
View Account Activity
in the navigation menu. See Figure 8.
Figure 8: Access Account Activity via the Member Profile Link
Complete the Account Activity page as follows. See Figure 9.
芒聢聮
Account
(mandatory) 芒聙聯 Use the drop-down menu to select an account. Select
All Accounts
to view account activity on all the customer芒聙聶s accounts.
芒聢聮
Show X most recent transactions in the last 15 days
(optional) 芒聙聯 Use the drop-down menu to select the maximum number of transactions that will be displayed in the search results.
芒聢聮
Date range
(optional) 芒聙聯 Select this option to search by date. Use the calendar icons to select a 芒聙聹From芒聙聺 date and a 芒聙聹To芒聙聺 date.
芒聢聮
Monthly
(optional) 芒聙聯 Select this option to view the customer芒聙聶s account activity for a specific month. Use the drop-down menu to select the month.
Figure 9: Search Account Activity
Select
Advanced Options
to select the following additional search criteria and display options (optional). See Figure 10.
芒聢聮
Show
芒聙聯 Use the Show drop-down menu to specify the transaction type that appears in the search results (e.g., deposits only) or to enter additional search criteria (e.g., search by amount).
芒聢聮
Format
芒聙聯 Use the Format drop-down menu to select how the search results will be displayed (e.g., on screen with details).
Figure 10: Advanced Search
Select
Search
. The search results are displayed.
7.5	Customer Alerts 芒聙聯 Add, Edit, or Delete Alerts
An alert is an automated text or email that is sent to a customer regarding activity on their account. Customers can set up alerts criteria via online banking or mobile app. However, if the customer attends in-branch, an authorized
PaymentStream
Direct user can view, add, or modify the customer芒聙聶s alerts at the customer芒聙聶s request. Authorization from another
PaymentStream
Direct user isn芒聙聶t required for these changes.
To view, add, edit, or delete the customer芒聙聶s alerts:
Access the customer芒聙聶s profile as described in Section 7.2, Accessing a Customer芒聙聶s Profile.
Select
View Member芒聙聶s Alert Settings
from the 芒聙聹Select a Task芒聙聺 drop-down menu. See Figure 11.
Figure 11: Select the View Member芒聙聶s Alert Settings Option
Alternatively, select
Member Profile
>
Manage Member Alerts
in the navigation menu. See Figure 12.
Figure 12: Access Manage Alerts via Member Profile Link
View the customer芒聙聶s active alert settings in the Your Active Member Alerts section. See Figure 13. See the following subsections for procedures on editing, deleting, or adding alerts.
Figure 13: View Alerts
Editing or Deleting Customer Alerts
To edit or delete alerts:
Select the applicable alert in the Your Active Member Alerts section. See Figure 13 above.
Select
Delete
to delete the alert. Confirm this action when prompted. Alternatively, select
Edit
to modify the alert setting. See Figure 14.
Figure 14: Edit or Delete an Alert
If editing, use the check boxes to select or remove the accounts to which an alert applies or to select or remove the contact to whom the alerts will be sent. Some alert types also allow users to change the frequency of reports, dollar amounts, and other self-explanatory variables. Select
Submit
. See Figure 15.
Figure 15: Editing an Alert
Adding Customer Alerts
To add an alert:
Go to the Add a New Member Alert section on the Alerts page and select the type
of alert that will be added (e.g., ATM Balance Inquiry). Select
Add
. See Figure 16.
Note: 	The Showing drop-down menu defaults to All Alerts. To restrict the type of new alerts that are displayed on the page, use this drop-down menu to select the appropriate alert type.
Figure 16: Select Alert Type
Depending on the type of alert added, select the accounts to which an alert applies, if applicable, and select the contact to whom the alerts will be sent. Some alert types also require the user to select the frequency of reports, dollar amount, and other self-explanatory variables. See Figure 17.
Note:	Links are available on this page to add email and mobile phone contact information for alerts.
Figure 17: Submit New Alert
Select
Submit
.
Note:	The Alerts Agreement appears when an alerts contact is added, not when an alert is added.
7.6	Customer Alerts 芒聙聯 Contacts and Account Nicknames
Customers can set up contact information and account nicknames for alerts via online banking or mobile app. However, if the customer attends in-branch, a
PaymentStream
Direct user can view, add, edit, or delete the contact information and account nicknames for alerts at the customer芒聙聶s request. Authorization from another
PaymentStream
Direct user isn芒聙聶t required for these changes.
To view, add, edit, or delete the customer芒聙聶s contact information or account nicknames for alerts:
Access the customer芒聙聶s profile as described in Section 7.2, Accessing a Customer芒聙聶s Profile.
Select
View Member芒聙聶s Alert Email, Phone and Account Nicknames
from the 芒聙聹Select a Task芒聙聺 drop-down menu. See Figure 18.
Figure 18: Select View Member芒聙聶s Alert Email, Phone and Account Nicknames
Alternatively, select
Member Profile
>
Manage Member Alerts
>
Member Alert Contacts and TextUs/Mobile Account Nicknames
in the navigation menu. See Figure 19.
Figure 19: Access Alerts Contact and Account Nicknames via the Member Profile Link
View the customer芒聙聶s contact information and account nicknames for alerts. See Figure 20. See the following subsections for procedures on adding, editing, or deleting the customer芒聙聶s contact information or account nicknames.
Figure 20: View Contacts and Account Nicknames
Adding the Customer芒聙聶s Contact Information
To add an email address or mobile phone number as an alerts contact:
Select
Add Email
to add a new email address.
Alternatively, select
Add Mobile Phone
to add a new mobile phone number. See Figure 21.
Figure 21: Select Add Email or Add Mobile Phone
Print the Alerts Agreement and have the customer review it. Alternatively, read the terms to the customer. Once the customer agrees, select
I accept
. See Figure 22.
Note:	The Alerts Agreement appears when an alerts contact is added, not when an alert is added.
Figure 22: Accept Agreement (Sample Agreement)
If the customer requested a new contact for email alerts, enter and confirm the email address when prompted. Select
Continue
. See Figure 23.
Figure 23: Enter Email Address
If the customer requested a new contact for text alerts, enter the mobile phone number and select the mobility carrier when prompted. Select
Continue
. See Figure 24. A passcode is automatically sent to the customer芒聙聶s mobile phone number.
Figure 24: Enter Mobile Phone Number
Request the passcode from the customer and enter it in the Passcode field. Select
Continue
to enable the phone number for alerts messages. See Figure 25.
Figure 25: Enter Passcode
Enabling, Disabling, or Deleting the Customer芒聙聶s Contact Information
To enable, disable, or delete contacts for alerts:
Select
Enable
,
Disable
, or
Delete
, as applicable, beside the appropriate contact. Only enabled contacts are available when adding or modifying alerts. See Figure 26.
Figure 26: Manage Alerts Contacts
Changing Account Nicknames
To change the customer芒聙聶s account nicknames for alerts:
Select
Edit Nicknames
. See Figure 27.
Figure 27: Edit Nicknames
Enter a new nickname beside the appropriate account and select
Submit
. See Figure 28.
Figure 28: Enter Account Nickname
7.7	Customer Alerts 芒聙聯 View Alerts History
The View Alerts History function allows authorized
PaymentStream
Direct users to view alerts sent to customers within the last 30 days. This information allows
PaymentStream
Direct users to answer customers芒聙聶 questions regarding alerts.
To view the customer芒聙聶s alerts history:
Access the customer芒聙聶s profile as described in Section 7.2, Accessing a Customer芒聙聶s Profile.
Select
View Member芒聙聶s Alert History
from the 芒聙聹Select a Task芒聙聺 drop-down menu. See Figure 29.
Figure 29: Select View Member芒聙聶s Alert History
Alternatively, select
Member Profile
>
Manage Member Alerts
>
View Member Alert History
in the navigation menu. See Figure 30.
Figure 30: Access Alerts History via Member Profile Link
View the customer芒聙聶s alerts history for the last 30 days. See Figure 31.
Figure 31: View Alerts History
7.8	Manage Mobile Banking
Mobile banking allows customers to conduct basic banking functions from their mobile phone via text messaging. Customers can set up mobile banking services via online banking. However, if the customer attends in-branch, an authorized
PaymentStream
Direct user can register a mobile phone for mobile banking at the customer芒聙聶s request.
To register a customer for mobile banking or to view or modify a customer芒聙聶s mobile banking settings:
Access the customer芒聙聶s profile as described in Section 7.2, Accessing a Customer芒聙聶s Profile.
Select
Register/Manage Member for Text Banking
from the 芒聙聹Select a Task芒聙聺 drop-down menu. See Figure 32.
Figure 32: Select Register/Manage Member for Text Banking
Alternatively, select
Member Profile
>
Manage Mobile Banking/TextUs
in the navigation menu. See Figure 33.
Figure 33: Access Mobile Banking via the Member Profile Link
If the customer previously registered a mobile phone, the customer芒聙聶s mobile banking settings are displayed in the Phone Management section. See Figure 34. See the following subsections for procedures on registering the customer for mobile banking or modifying the customer芒聙聶s mobile banking settings.
Note:	To view text commands, select
View Available TextUs Commands
.
Figure 34: View Mobile Banking Settings
Registering a Customer for Mobile Banking
To register a phone for mobile banking:
Select
Add Mobile Phone
if the customer previously registered another mobile phone. See Figure 35.
Note:	The user can register a maximum of two mobile phone numbers for mobile banking. The Add Mobile Phone button doesn芒聙聶t appear if two phones are already registered. Delete one if necessary.
Figure 35: Add Mobile Phone
Alternatively, if the customer hasn芒聙聶t registered a phone for mobile banking, select
Register your phone for Mobile Banking
. See Figure 36.
Figure 36: Register for Mobile Banking
Print the Mobile Banking/TextUs User Agreement and have the customer review it. Alternatively, read the terms to the customer. Once the customer agrees, select
Accept
. See Figure 37.
Figure 37: Accept Agreement
Enter the phone number and select the mobility carrier when prompted. Select
Continue
. See Figure 38. A passcode is automatically sent to the customer芒聙聶s mobile phone number.
Figure 38: Enter Mobile Phone Number
Request the passcode from the customer and enter it in the Passcode field. Select
Continue
to enable the phone number for mobile banking. See Figure 39.
Figure 39: Enter Passcode
Select the accounts that will be accessible via mobile banking, edit the account nicknames, if applicable, and select a primary account. See Figure 40.
Figure 40: Select Accounts for Mobile Banking Access
Enabling, Disabling, or Deleting a Phone for Mobile Banking
To enable, disable, or delete a phone for mobile banking:
Select
Enable
,
Disable
, or
Delete
, as applicable, beside the appropriate phone number on the Manage Mobile Baking/TextUs 芒聙聯 Preferences page. Only enabled phone numbers are available for mobile banking. See Figure 41.
Figure 41: Manage Mobile Banking Phone Numbers
Changing Accounts Accessible via Mobile Banking and Editing Account Nicknames
To change the accounts that are accessible via mobile banking or to edit the account nicknames:
Select
Edit
on the Manage Mobile Baking/Text Us 芒聙聯 Preferences page. See Figure 42.
Figure 42: Select Edit
Update the nickname of the account, the accounts to which the customer requires mobile banking access, or the customer芒聙聶s preferred primary account, as required. Select
Confirm
. See Figure 43.
Figure 43: Edit Mobile Banking Account Settings
7.9	PAN Change
This section applies to financial institutions that use a PAN to identify users for online banking. When a new Member Card
脗庐
debit card with a different PAN is issued (e.g., when a card is lost or stolen), your financial institution can update the PAN in
MemberDirect
脗庐
to reduce the impact to the customer. If the new PAN isn芒聙聶t changed in
MemberDirect
, your customer may need to perform some tasks manually (e.g., re-register for mobile banking).
The
PaymentStream
Direct PAN Change function allows authorized users to update
MemberDirect
Integrated (MDI) and
MemberDirect
Online (MDO) with a new PAN. The PAN Change function can be used in lieu of the manual batch PAN change process.
Note:	Follow existing policies and processes when issuing replacement Member Card debit cards (e.g., verify the customer芒聙聶s identity).
To update
MemberDirect
with a new PAN via
PaymentStream
Direct:
Log in to
PaymentStream
Direct and select the Transactions tab if that page isn芒聙聶t automatically displayed.
Select
Change Member芒聙聶s PAN
from the 芒聙聹Select a Task芒聙聺 drop-down menu. See Figure 44.
Note:	Since it isn芒聙聶t necessary to be in a customer-serving state to use the PAN Change function, the user doesn芒聙聶t need to access the customer芒聙聶s account.
Figure 44: Select the Change Customer芒聙聶s PAN Option
Alternatively, select
Member Profile
>
PAN Change
in the navigation menu. See Figure 45.
Figure 45: Alternate Access to PAN Change Function
Enter the old Member Card number and the new Member Card number, then select
Submit
. See Figure 46.
Figure 46: Enter Card Numbers
Select
Submit
to confirm the change. See Figure 47.
Figure 47: Confirm Change
The receipt is displayed. To print a copy of the receipt for the customer, select
Print Receipt
. See Figure 48.
Figure 48: Print Receipt
7.10	Downloading e-Statements
The Downloading e-Statements function allows authorized
PaymentStream
Direct users to view and print statements for customers. The teller selects the date of the e-statement. A PDF is generated. The teller can print a copy of the statement for the customer.
To download and print an e-statement:
Access the customer芒聙聶s profile as described in Section 7.2, Accessing a Customer芒聙聶s Profile.
Select
View Member芒聙聶s e-Statements
from the 芒聙聹Select a Task芒聙聺 drop-down menu. See Figure 49.
Figure 49: View Member芒聙聶s e-Statements
Alternatively, select
Member Profile
>
View e-Statements
in the navigation menu. This information is always visible while working in a customer-serving state.
Select the year and then select the monthly statement to download. See Figure 50.
Figure 50: Select e-Statement Date
Select
Open
when prompted to open or save the statement.
A statement lists all the customer芒聙聶s accounts and shows the transactions for each account in that month. See Figure 51.
Figure 51: Sample e-Statement
7.11	Customer Messages
The Enhanced Personal Message feature is an optional
MemberDirect
Integrated Services function that allows you to send messages to your customers. The
PaymentStream
Direct Member Messages function allows users to view, print, or delete these messages.
To view customer messages:
Access the customer芒聙聶s profile as described in Section 7.2, Accessing a Customer芒聙聶s Profile.
Select the 芒聙聹Select a Task芒聙聺 drop-down arrow and select
View Member芒聙聶s Messages
. See Figure 52.
Figure 52: View Member芒聙聶s Messages
In the Member Messages pane, the messages are categorized as:
Urgent Messages
Financial Reminders
Personal Messages
Users can open messages or delete old messages. Unread messages are displayed in bold. To open a message, select
Read More
. See Figure 53.
Figure 53: Member Messages Pane
END
